title: Adversarial Robustness
existing_short_definition: A model's resistance to carefully crafted inputs designed to cause misclassification or harmful output — a classic ML security concern extended to LLMs.

title: AI Governance
existing_short_definition: The policies, procedures, and oversight mechanisms an organization uses to manage AI systems — from risk assessments to access controls to audit trails.

title: Alignment (AI Alignment)
existing_short_definition: The research problem of ensuring AI systems pursue human-intended goals safely, honestly, and helpfully — the umbrella term for RLHF, DPO, Constitutional AI, and related work.

title: Alignment Tax
existing_short_definition: The performance cost a model pays on general capabilities after alignment training — the trade-off between being useful and being safe/helpful.

title: Backdoor Attack
existing_short_definition: A data-poisoning attack that makes a model behave normally except when a specific 'trigger' pattern is present, at which point it misbehaves.

title: C2PA (Content Provenance Standard)
existing_short_definition: An open standard for cryptographically signing content metadata — who made it, when, with what tool — to establish provenance.

title: CaMeL (Google Prompt Injection Defense)
existing_short_definition: Google's 'Capability-Based Mitigation for LLMs' — a system that separates trusted instructions from untrusted data using a privileged planning model and a restricted execution model.

title: Content Moderation (AI-Based)
existing_short_definition: Using ML systems — toxicity classifiers, LLM-based reviewers, image/video analyzers — to review user-generated or AI-generated content against policy.

title: Data Poisoning
existing_short_definition: An attack where an adversary injects malicious examples into training data to embed a backdoor, degrade quality, or cause specific misbehavior at inference time.

title: Datasheet (for Datasets)
existing_short_definition: A structured document describing a dataset's composition, collection process, intended uses, and known limitations — the dataset equivalent of a model card.

title: Deepfake Detection
existing_short_definition: Techniques for detecting AI-generated or AI-manipulated images, audio, and video — a moving target as generators improve.

title: Differential Privacy
existing_short_definition: A mathematical privacy guarantee that the output of a computation reveals essentially nothing about any single input example.

title: EU AI Act
existing_short_definition: The European Union's comprehensive AI regulation (2024) that classifies AI systems by risk tier and imposes obligations ranging from transparency to prohibitions.

title: Goal Misgeneralization
existing_short_definition: When an agent learns to pursue a goal that is correlated with the intended goal on training data but diverges in deployment.

title: Guardrails
existing_short_definition: Runtime filters and validators that wrap an LLM call to enforce output constraints — schema, safety, factuality, PII removal — and re-ask on failure.
existing_code_example:
# Guardrails-AI with Pydantic schema
from guardrails import Guard
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class Output(BaseModel):
    sentiment: Literal['pos','neu','neg']
    summary: str = Field(max_length=160)

guard = Guard.for_pydantic(Output)
result = guard(llm, prompt='analyze this review...', num_reasks=2)

title: Guardrails AI
existing_short_definition: An open-source Python framework for enforcing structured output and policy compliance on LLM responses via validators and re-prompts.

title: Hallucination
existing_short_definition: When an AI confidently states something that's completely false.

title: Hallucination Detection
existing_short_definition: Automated methods for flagging LLM outputs that are not grounded in evidence — via self-consistency, citation checking, or entailment classifiers.

title: Indirect Prompt Injection
existing_short_definition: A prompt injection attack delivered through content the LLM reads (a web page, email, document) rather than the direct user input.

title: Jailbreak
existing_short_definition: A prompt crafted to make an aligned LLM bypass its safety training and produce content it was trained to refuse.

title: Jailbreak Defense
existing_short_definition: Techniques for hardening LLMs against user attempts to bypass safety guardrails — from training (RLHF, constitutional AI) to inference (input/output filtering, classifiers).

title: Llama Guard
existing_short_definition: Meta's open-source LLM-based safety classifier trained to detect policy violations in both user prompts and model responses.

title: Membership Inference Attack
existing_short_definition: An attack that determines whether a specific example was part of a model's training data — a privacy violation even without full data extraction.

title: Model Spec (OpenAI)
existing_short_definition: OpenAI's public document describing intended model behavior — rules, defaults, and principles for how ChatGPT and API models should respond.

title: Model Theft (Model Extraction)
existing_short_definition: An attack that reconstructs or approximates a closed-API model by querying it extensively and training a student on the outputs.

title: NeMo Guardrails
existing_short_definition: Nvidia's open-source framework for adding programmable safety and topical guardrails to LLM applications via a declarative DSL (Colang).

title: AI Safety Institute / NIST AI RMF
existing_short_definition: NIST's AI Risk Management Framework — a voluntary structured approach to identifying, assessing, and mitigating AI system risks used increasingly by US regulators.

title: Over-Refusal
existing_short_definition: When a safety-tuned LLM refuses benign requests it misclassifies as harmful — a common failure mode of aggressive alignment.

title: PII Redaction
existing_short_definition: Detecting and removing Personally Identifiable Information (names, emails, SSN, addresses) from text before logging, storing, or sending to external LLM APIs.
existing_code_example:
from presidio_analyzer import AnalyzerEngine
from presidio_anonymizer import AnonymizerEngine

analyzer, anonymizer = AnalyzerEngine(), AnonymizerEngine()
results = analyzer.analyze(text, language='en')
clean = anonymizer.anonymize(text=text, analyzer_results=results).text

title: Prompt Injection
existing_short_definition: An attack where malicious text in user input or retrieved content overrides the system's original instructions to the LLM.

title: Prompt Injection Defense
existing_short_definition: Techniques for hardening LLM apps against prompt-injection attacks — input sanitization, role-based delimiting, output monitoring, and tool-permission least privilege.

title: Prompt Leakage (System Prompt Exfiltration)
existing_short_definition: An attack that recovers a system prompt by tricking the LLM into repeating its hidden instructions.

title: Red Teaming (AI Red Team)
existing_short_definition: Adversarial testing of an AI system by human or automated attackers to find vulnerabilities, harmful outputs, and policy violations before deployment.

title: Reward Hacking
existing_short_definition: A failure mode of RL where the model learns to maximize the reward signal in ways that violate the designer's intent.

title: Specification Gaming
existing_short_definition: A broader term for agents that satisfy the letter of a specification while violating its intent — reward hacking is one instance.

title: Spotlighting (Prompt Injection Defense)
existing_short_definition: A prompt-injection defense that transforms untrusted content into a form (tagging, encoding) making instructions within it harder for the model to follow.

title: Supply Chain Risk (AI)
existing_short_definition: The risk that downloaded models, datasets, or training pipelines contain backdoors, poisoned data, or undisclosed behaviors — a growing concern as open-weights use expands.

title: Sycophancy
existing_short_definition: An LLM's tendency to agree with users rather than tell them uncomfortable truths, often reinforced by RLHF on preference data that rewards flattery.

title: Toxicity Classification
existing_short_definition: ML systems that score text (and sometimes images) for harmful, abusive, or policy-violating content for moderation pipelines.

title: Training Data Extraction
existing_short_definition: An attack that elicits verbatim training data from a model's outputs — a major privacy and copyright concern.

title: Content Watermarking (AI Content Provenance)
existing_short_definition: Techniques for marking AI-generated content so it can later be identified — steganographic for images, statistical for text.
